MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-01-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS MARJOLAINE AVERY, NOELLE AVERY AND DJESON AVERY'S NOTICE OF HEARING FOR PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM CLAIM REQUIREMENT [PURSUANT TO CAL. GOVT. CODE SECTION 946.6]. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - PETITION IS DENIED. POLICE INCIDENT REPORT STATES OFFICER LAM SPOTTED THE SUSPECT, TURNED ON HIS LIGHTS WHILE MAKING A U-TURN, TURNED OFF HIS LIGHTS AND FOLLOWED THE SUSPECT FOR APPROXIMATELY SIX BLOCKS BEFORE THE ACCIDENT. THE INFORMATION SUGGESTS THAT OFFICER LAM MAY HAVE BEEN PARTIALLY RESPONSIBLE FOR THE ACCIDENT AND TRIGGERED THE PETITIONERS' DUTY TO INVESTIGATE. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT PETITIONERS WERE PREVENTED FROM CONDUCTING A REASONABLE INVESTIGATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: TAMRA KEEN, CSR #5404
ORDER DENYING PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM CLAIM REQUIREMENT

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing For Relief From Claim Requirement
Off Calendar Apr-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONERS MARJOLAINE AVERY, NOELLE AVERY and DJESON AVERY'S Notice Of Hearing for Petition For Relief From Claim Requirement [Pursuant to Cal. Govt. Code Section 946.6].
APPEARANCES: James Parks, Esq. of Devito Law Group 421-2154 for petitioners & moving parties; Blake P. Loebs, Deputy City Atty 554-3868 554-3868 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Petition is denied. Police incident report states Officer Lam spotted the suspect, turned on his lights while making a U-turn, turned off his lights and followed the suspect for approximately six blocks before the accident. The information suggests that Officer Lam may have been partially responsible for the accident and triggered the petitioners' duty to investigate. Insufficient evidence presented that Petitioners were prevented from conducting a reasonable investigation. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW/VC) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: TAMRA KEEN, CSR #5404

Notice Of Hearing For Relief From Claim Requirement
Off Calendar Apr-01-2009 Continued To Apr-23-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to April 23, 2009. Moving Party to provide courtesy copies with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (302PHA/VC)Judge: Paul H.
